acceptance_criteria.criterion: skills/review.md explicitly states: each question requiring user input is independent — a 'yes' to one question does not carry over to subsequent questions
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Lines 53-54 of review.md: 'Each question requiring user input is an independent decision — a "yes" to one question does NOT carry over to subsequent questions.' Exact wording matches the AC.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The supervised transition protocol in review.md separates archive approval from TODO extraction approval as distinct user consent gates
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Lines 55-56 of review.md: 'Archive approval, TODO extraction, learning promotion, and any other user-facing decisions are separate consent gates.' Explicitly names archive and TODO extraction as distinct gates, and extends the principle to learning promotion.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Anti-pattern documented: borrowing consent from a prior unrelated user response to skip a subsequent decision point
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Lines 57-59 of review.md: 'Do not interpret prior user responses as approval for unrelated subsequent decisions (e.g., "yes to archive" does not mean "yes to skip TODOs" or "yes to promote learnings").' Anti-pattern documented with concrete examples.

dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Instructions accurately describe the desired consent isolation behavior. The principle (independent decisions), the named gates (archive, TODO extraction, learning promotion), and the anti-pattern (consent borrowing) all align with the objective in definition.yaml.

dimensions.name: maintainability
dimensions.evidence: Consent Isolation is a 7-line subsection under the Supervised Transition Protocol — exactly where a reader would look for consent rules. No duplication with other sections. Uses concrete examples instead of abstract language.

dimensions.name: api_contract
dimensions.evidence: Consent isolation lives only in review.md, which is appropriate: review is the only step with multiple sequential user decisions (archive, TODOs, learnings). Other step skills have a single transition gate and do not need this section. No cross-file inconsistency.

dimensions.name: completeness
dimensions.evidence: All three consent-requiring actions in the review flow are named: archive approval, TODO extraction, and learning promotion. The anti-pattern section covers the general principle and gives two specific forbidden inference examples.

dimensions.name: implementability
dimensions.evidence: Instructions are unambiguous and mechanically verifiable: an agent either asks each question independently or it does not. The negative examples ('yes to archive does not mean yes to skip TODOs') make the boundary clear without requiring interpretation.
